subject: South Washington - Food Required pulling incorrect stock item

turns.author: Erica Pawletzki <epawletz@sowashco.org>
turns.body_text: Erica Pawletzki                    Where does the 'Food Required' part of a production plan/technician's worksheet pull from?           08:45 AM, 05th Aug                     Erica Pawletzki                    It is showing incorrectly on a few recipes, but we have confirmed that the recipe/ingredient/stock item are all correct and linked           08:46 AM, 05th Aug                     Erica Pawletzki                    The correct product does pull into their orders, but shows incorrectly on Technician Worksheet           08:47 AM, 05th Aug                     Erica Pawletzki                    Menu Item: Crispy Chicken Bowl (TR1689) is an example of this. Market Square line on Mondays at the High Schools           08:47 AM, 05th Aug                   Terri                   Good Morning  - the food required pulls from the stock item linked to the ingredients of the recipes.  When troubleshooting, you always want to make sure the broken unit weight is accurate           08:51 AM, 05th Aug                   Terri                   Let me check on TR1689           08:52 AM, 05th Aug                     Erica Pawletzki                    Thank you           08:55 AM, 05th Aug                   Terri                   I see the serving measure is 5 ounce on the menu that was assigned, which would be 5.73 each based on the weight measures - the recipe is currently set at a 4.365 ounce which is 5 each.           09:12 AM, 05th Aug                   Terri                   I know you have the first week planned counts are complete, but you may want to correct the menu itself to the 5 each serving measure (or the 4.365 ounce) for the math to come out correctly on the food required           09:14 AM, 05th Aug                   Terri                   Once you have the menu corrected, you can assign again           09:15 AM, 05th Aug                     Erica Pawletzki                    The problem is that the Technician sheet is showing a completely (wrong) product from what the recipe is using as a stock item.           09:16 AM, 05th Aug                     Erica Pawletzki                    When they order through production is pulls in the correct stock item number, but when they print out their Technician sheet it shows a different stock item number which is confusing them           09:17 AM, 05th Aug                   Terri                            09:18 AM, 05th Aug                   Terri                            09:18 AM, 05th Aug                   Terri                   I am seeing the same stock item           09:18 AM, 05th Aug                     Erica Pawletzki                    Yes, but look in the recipe and the ingredient and the stock item it's linked to and you'll see a different number.           09:19 AM, 05th Aug                     Erica Pawletzki                    We don't know how that's possible           09:19 AM, 05th Aug                   Terri                   Ok, I used the "Update Food Required" button on both the stock item and the recipe and now it is showing the correct stock item - I did also re-save the production plans           09:25 AM, 05th Aug                   Terri                   When they complete their plans in the future, the correct stock item should pull through for you as well           09:26 AM, 05th Aug                     Erica Pawletzki                    Thank you bunches!           09:28 AM, 05th Aug                   Terri                   my pleasure!           09:29 AM, 05th Aug                                          Take to Team Inbox
turns.body_html:
Erica Pawletzki
Where does the 'Food Required' part of a production plan/technician's worksheet pull from?
08:45 AM, 05th Aug
Erica Pawletzki
It is showing incorrectly on a few recipes, but we have confirmed that the recipe/ingredient/stock item are all correct and linked
08:46 AM, 05th Aug
Erica Pawletzki
The correct product does pull into their orders, but shows incorrectly on Technician Worksheet
08:47 AM, 05th Aug
Erica Pawletzki
Menu Item: Crispy Chicken Bowl (TR1689) is an example of this. Market Square line on Mondays at the High Schools
08:47 AM, 05th Aug
Terri
Good Morning - the food required pulls from the stock item linked to the ingredients of the recipes. When troubleshooting, you always want to make sure the broken unit weight is accurate
08:51 AM, 05th Aug
Terri
Let me check on TR1689
08:52 AM, 05th Aug
Erica Pawletzki
Thank you
08:55 AM, 05th Aug
Terri
I see the serving measure is 5 ounce on the menu that was assigned, which would be 5.73 each based on the weight measures - the recipe is currently set at a 4.365 ounce which is 5 each.
09:12 AM, 05th Aug
Terri
I know you have the first week planned counts are complete, but you may want to correct the menu itself to the 5 each serving measure (or the 4.365 ounce) for the math to come out correctly on the food required
09:14 AM, 05th Aug
Terri
Once you have the menu corrected, you can assign again
09:15 AM, 05th Aug
Erica Pawletzki
The problem is that the Technician sheet is showing a completely (wrong) product from what the recipe is using as a stock item.
09:16 AM, 05th Aug
Erica Pawletzki
When they order through production is pulls in the correct stock item number, but when they print out their Technician sheet it shows a different stock item number which is confusing them
09:17 AM, 05th Aug
Terri
09:18 AM, 05th Aug
Terri
09:18 AM, 05th Aug
Terri
I am seeing the same stock item
09:18 AM, 05th Aug
Erica Pawletzki
Yes, but look in the recipe and the ingredient and the stock item it's linked to and you'll see a different number.
09:19 AM, 05th Aug
Erica Pawletzki
We don't know how that's possible
09:19 AM, 05th Aug
Terri
Ok, I used the "Update Food Required" button on both the stock item and the recipe and now it is showing the correct stock item - I did also re-save the production plans
09:25 AM, 05th Aug
Terri
When they complete their plans in the future, the correct stock item should pull through for you as well
09:26 AM, 05th Aug
Erica Pawletzki
Thank you bunches!
09:28 AM, 05th Aug
Terri
my pleasure!
09:29 AM, 05th Aug
Take to Team Inbox

subject: Wichita 0 Recipe Weight Off

turns.author: Adrea Katzenmeier <akatzenmeier@usd259.net>
turns.body_text: I am looking at LI100066 Cheese Cheddar Cubes Bulk. LR1222.   When this product is inside a recipe, the amount needed is not correct. It is always showing "(250lb. 7.05oz)" when we scale recipes to 1000; no mater the actual qty needed in case/bag amounts.     Examples below.    LR1390, scaled to 1000 servings.  Need 3 cases and 0.522 bags of Cheddar Cheese Cubes (which is about 62 pounds total).  The system is saying 250lb. 7.05oz under quantity.    LR2165, scaled to 1000 servings.  Need 6 case and 1.045 bag (about 125# total).  The system is saying (250lb 7.05oz)    LR2131, scaled to 1000 servings. Need 5 case and 0.036 bag (about 100# total). System is saying (250lb 7.05oz)       Ingredient case, bag and ¼ cup weights are set up correctly in ingredient.  Stock number looks correct.  Recipe looks correct.    Please Advise.                    Adrea Katzenmeier, RD, SNS  Assistant Director  Nutrition Services          Wichita Public Schools  3850 N. Hydraulic | Wichita, KS | 67219  P: 316-973-2211  F: 316-973-2162 www.usd259.org/nutrition      If you or someone you know is interested in joining our Nutrition Services team to help feed Wichita kids, learn more information about our open positions here.
turns.body_html:
I am looking at LI100066 Cheese Cheddar Cubes Bulk. LR1222.   When this product is inside a recipe, the amount needed is not correct. It is always showing "(250lb. 7.05oz)" when we scale recipes to 1000; no mater the actual qty needed in case/bag amounts.

Examples below.
  1. LR1390, scaled to 1000 servings.  Need 3 cases and 0.522 bags of Cheddar Cheese Cubes (which is about 62 pounds total).  The system is saying 250lb. 7.05oz under quantity.
  2. LR2165, scaled to 1000 servings.  Need 6 case and 1.045 bag (about 125# total).  The system is saying (250lb 7.05oz)
  3. LR2131, scaled to 1000 servings. Need 5 case and 0.036 bag (about 100# total). System is saying (250lb 7.05oz)

Ingredient case, bag and ¼ cup weights are set up correctly in ingredient.  Stock number looks correct.  Recipe looks correct.  
Please Advise.


 
Adrea Katzenmeier, RD, SNS
Assistant Director
Nutrition Services

 
Wichita Public Schools
3850 N. Hydraulic | Wichita, KS | 67219
 P: 316-973-2211
 F: 316-973-2162
www.usd259.org/nutrition 
 
If you or someone you know is interested in joining our Nutrition Services team to help feed Wichita kids, learn more information about our open positions here.

subject: Port Arthur ISD - Question

turns.author: Meshala Huff <meshala.huff@paisd.org>
turns.body_text:
CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.
 
 
 
 
 Good afternoon, 
 
 
 
 
 If a single ingredient recipe is linked to a stock item and an ingredient, why would it not show up under food required on the production plans? 
 
 
 
 Thank you, 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Meshala Huff 
 Assistant Director of Child Nutrition 
Email: meshala.huff@paisd.org
 Phone: 409-984-6231 
 Cell: 409-960-2745 
 
 
 
 
 
 
  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
CONFIDENTIALITY NOTICE:  This message from the Port Arthur Independent School District may contain confidential or privileged information. If you received this transmission in error,
 please return it to the sender and delete the material from any computer. Disclosure or use of any part of this message by persons other than the intended recipient is prohibited.
turns.body_html:
CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.
Good afternoon,

If a single ingredient recipe is linked to a stock item and an ingredient, why would it not show up under food required on the production plans?

Thank you,


Meshala Huff
Assistant Director of Child Nutrition
Email: meshala.huff@paisd.org
Phone: 409-984-6231
Cell: 409-960-2745

turns.author: "customercare@primeroedge.com" <customercare@primeroedge.com>
turns.body_text:
Hello Meshala,  This could be due to the single ingredient recipe tied to a stock item that does not have a broken unit weight under the buying guide tab of the ingredient that would be preventing the system from properly calculating the amount needed. If it does have a broken unit, then make sure the recipe also has show in summary checked so the item can be shown in the production plan food required. If both are done, then let me know and also provide the recipe code if it is a specific single ingredient recipe that is giving issues. (the pictures shown below are just examples for clarity). turns.body_html:
Shelley Ingram
I have just started uploading my DC files. Our Certified Reduced students are a different file. Will I upload it the same?
09:20 AM, 17th Oct
Keith
I am not following that. A DC file should contain all your DC's not just Free ones
09:21 AM, 17th Oct
Keith
Why do you have 2 diff. files?
09:21 AM, 17th Oct
Shelley Ingram
I do not know. I just know that on our FL DC it is two downloads.
09:22 AM, 17th Oct
Shelley Ingram
When I looked at our student info (FOCUS) the students on the FDC reduced list are listed as Paid
09:23 AM, 17th Oct
Keith
This is the DC job that is set up for you right now. There is only one job and one file.
09:27 AM, 17th Oct
Shelley Ingram
Is there a suggestion?
09:27 AM, 17th Oct
Keith
and you processed it last time so what is different this time
09:27 AM, 17th Oct
Keith
just do the same thing you did last month
09:28 AM, 17th Oct
Shelley Ingram
So I can just do it again, but with the Certified Reduced list only
09:28 AM, 17th Oct
Keith
Yes, it is not as easy, but you can do that.
09:29 AM, 17th Oct
Keith
1. Recalculate the Weights
Often, if you've made changes to the ingredients, the system needs to be prompted to update the total weight.

Navigate to the Ingredients tab of the recipe.

Click the (Re)calculate Weights button. This will recalculate the nutrient analysis and should update the total weight on the General tab.

You can also use (Re)calculate all Purchase Items if you've changed ingredients or steps.

2. Verify Ingredient Information
The "Recipe Total Weight" on the General tab is automatically calculated based on the ingredients you've entered. An error here is often traced back to an incorrect entry on the Ingredients tab.

Go to the Ingredients tab.

Carefully review each ingredient listed.

Check that the Quantity and the Unit of Measure (e.g., patty, slice, cup) are correct for each ingredient. A simple typo here is a common culprit.

3. Check for Moisture, Fat, and Waste Factors
Certain cooking processes can change the weight of the final product. PrimeroEdge has fields to account for this.

On the General tab of the recipe, look for the fields for Moisture and Fat Change (%) and Waste Factor.

These fields are optional but crucial for accuracy with certain foods.

Moisture/Fat: For example, pasta gains moisture and weight when cooked, while ground beef loses fat and weight.

Waste: An item like a watermelon will have waste from the rind that should be accounted for.

Ensure these fields are filled in correctly if they apply to your recipe.
09:30 AM, 17th Oct
Keith
whoops wrong spot
09:30 AM, 17th Oct
Keith
ignore this
09:30 AM, 17th Oct
Shelley Ingram
Can you email me the instructions and copy rherrington@ettpro.net
09:42 AM, 17th Oct
Shelley Ingram
singram@fcsdfl.org
09:42 AM, 17th Oct
Keith
Sure give me a few moments
09:42 AM, 17th Oct
Shelley Ingram
Thank you
09:50 AM, 17th Oct
Take to Team Inbox

1. Recalculate the Weights
Often, if you've made changes to the ingredients, the system needs to be prompted to update the total weight.

Navigate to the Ingredients tab of the recipe.

Click the (Re)calculate Weights button. This will recalculate the nutrient analysis and should update the total weight on the General tab.

You can also use (Re)calculate all Purchase Items if you've changed ingredients or steps.

2. Verify Ingredient Information
The "Recipe Total Weight" on the General tab is automatically calculated based on the ingredients you've entered. An error here is often traced back to an incorrect entry on the Ingredients tab.

Go to the Ingredients tab.

Carefully review each ingredient listed.

Check that the Quantity and the Unit of Measure (e.g., patty, slice, cup) are correct for each ingredient. A simple typo here is a common culprit.

3. Check for Moisture, Fat, and Waste Factors
Certain cooking processes can change the weight of the final product. PrimeroEdge has fields to account for this.

On the General tab of the recipe, look for the fields for Moisture and Fat Change (%) and Waste Factor.

These fields are optional but crucial for accuracy with certain foods.

Moisture/Fat: For example, pasta gains moisture and weight when cooked, while ground beef loses fat and weight.

Waste: An item like a watermelon will have waste from the rind that should be accounted for.

Ensure these fields are filled in correctly if they apply to your recipe.

turns.author: "customercare@primeroedge.com" <customercare@primeroedge.com>
turns.body_text:
Hello Tanya,     You should be able to click the pencil next to the 1/4 cup to edit the grams there, and it will flow through to the nutrients for your 2 teaspoons.     There are 12 teaspoons in a 1/4 cup.  Based on your statement that 2 teaspoons should be 5 grams, that means that each teaspoon is 2.5 grams.  12 teaspoons at 2.5 grams = 30 grams  So changing the 1/4 cup to 30 grams should update the nutrients tab to the 5 grams that you're expecting.          ✅ Complete your Year Begin Tasks ✨ Explore PrimeroEdge’s fresh look: reimagined.primeroedge.com ? See the Navigation

replying all to this ticket          Schrenia Bockholt | CYBERSOFT  Business Analyst, Product Management  PrimeroEdge | SchoolCafé
turns.body_html:
Hello Tanya,

You should be able to click the pencil next to the 1/4 cup to edit the grams there, and it will flow through to the nutrients for your 2 teaspoons.

There are 12 teaspoons in a 1/4 cup.
Based on your statement that 2 teaspoons should be 5 grams, that means that each teaspoon is 2.5 grams.
12 teaspoons at 2.5 grams = 30 grams
So changing the 1/4 cup to 30 grams should update the nutrients tab to the 5 grams that you're expecting.

turns.author: agent:22004298836
turns.body_text: Everything looks normal on the page - so I'm not sure why she would not be able to enter nutrients for the ingredient.  Make sure she's entering 1 and selecting "Slice" from the drop down at the top of the nutrients listings on that tab.      Additionally - when you're talking to her - point out that her "Quantity" measurement on the Recipe Tab is roughly 17 servings, but she has 1 serving entered.   If she changes that quantity to "1 Pie" or "43 Ounce" - then her Number of servings would be 10.  According to the document she has attached on the "Documents" tab, each pie is 43 ounces, so I'm not sure where here 76 1/4 is coming from.
turns.body_html:
Everything looks normal on the page - so I'm not sure why she would not be able to enter nutrients for the ingredient.
Make sure she's entering 1 and selecting "Slice" from the drop down at the top of the nutrients listings on that tab. 

Additionally - when you're talking to her - point out that her "Quantity" measurement on the Recipe Tab is roughly 17 servings, but she has 1 serving entered. 
If she changes that quantity to "1 Pie" or "43 Ounce" - then her Number of servings would be 10.
According to the document she has attached on the "Documents" tab, each pie is 43 ounces, so I'm not sure where here 76 1/4 is coming from.

turns.author: Sandy Bland <1067609418083309@freshchat.com>
turns.body_text: Sandy Bland                    How to delete an ingredient from a recipe?           11:53 AM, 17th Nov                   Keith                   Navigate to the Recipe:  Go to Menu Planning > Ingredients > Recipes.  Search for and select the recipe you wish to modify.  Access the Ingredients Tab:  Click on the Ingredients tab within the recipe record.  Delete the Ingredient:  Locate the list of Steps (ingredients) added to the recipe.  Find the specific ingredient you want to remove.  Click the Delete icon (typically represented by a trash can or delete symbol) in the row corresponding to that ingredient.  Save Changes:  Confirm the deletion if prompted.  Ensure you Save the recipe to apply the changes permanently.           11:56 AM, 17th Nov                                          Take to Team Inbox
turns.body_html:
Sandy Bland
How to delete an ingredient from a recipe?
11:53 AM, 17th Nov
Keith
Navigate to the Recipe:

Go to Menu Planning > Ingredients > Recipes.

Search for and select the recipe you wish to modify.

Access the Ingredients Tab:

Click on the Ingredients tab within the recipe record.

Delete the Ingredient:

Locate the list of Steps (ingredients) added to the recipe.

Find the specific ingredient you want to remove.

Click the Delete icon (typically represented by a trash can or delete symbol) in the row corresponding to that ingredient.

Save Changes:

Confirm the deletion if prompted.

Ensure you Save the recipe to apply the changes permanently.

turns.author: agent:22052027946
turns.body_text: I will check with Marcus before responding to determine if it's the correct solution. Additionally, I will ask him whether I should update the setting and inform them in such cases or request that the district update it themselves.      Testing in Online 5 UAT:     When the "Method to convert Ingredient Measurements" setting for recipes is set to "Use Standard Measurements," the recipe displays paprika measurements in cups and tablespoons.               Updated the setting "Use Preferred Measurement," and the recipe report is now working as expected.
turns.body_html:
I will check with Marcus before responding to determine if it's the correct solution. Additionally, I will ask him whether I should update the setting and inform them in such cases or request that the district update it themselves. 

Testing in Online 5 UAT:

When the "Method to convert Ingredient Measurements" setting for recipes is set to "Use Standard Measurements," the recipe displays paprika measurements in cups and tablespoons.



Updated the setting "Use Preferred Measurement," and the recipe report is now working as expected.

turns.author: agent:22052027946
turns.body_text: Spoke to Marcus and he approved
turns.body_html: Spoke to Marcus and he approved

turns.author: agent:22052027946
turns.body_text: This issue can be resolved by updating a setting. Please follow these steps to resolve the issue:      Navigate to the system settings.  Click on "System Settings."  Select "Menu Planning."  Search for the setting "Method to convert Ingredient Measurements" and update it from "Use Standard Measurements" to "Use Preferred Measurement," then click "Save."  Return to the recipe report and refresh it. The report should now display paprika quantity in tablespoons.               Please follow the steps outlined above, update the settings accordingly, and verify the changes. If you need any further assistance, please let us know.
turns.body_html:
This issue can be resolved by updating a setting. Please follow these steps to resolve the issue:

  1. Navigate to the system settings.
  2. Click on "System Settings."
  3. Select "Menu Planning."
  4. Search for the setting "Method to convert Ingredient Measurements" and update it from "Use Standard Measurements" to "Use Preferred Measurement," then click "Save."
  5. Return to the recipe report and refresh it. The report should now display paprika quantity in tablespoons.




Please follow the steps outlined above, update the settings accordingly, and verify the changes. If you need any further assistance, please let us know.

subject: Stillwater Area - Ingredient Cost not pulling correctly

turns.author: Brandon Porter <porterb@stillwaterschools.org>
turns.body_text: Brandon Porter                    Any one here to help?           10:35 AM, 24th Nov                   Jared                   Thank you for contacting customer care. I hope you're having a great day!           10:36 AM, 24th Nov                   Jared                   What can I do for you today           10:36 AM, 24th Nov                     Brandon Porter                    i have an ingredient in a recipe that wont price correctly           10:36 AM, 24th Nov                   Terri                   Hi Brandon - What is the ingredient code?  And the recipe code?           10:37 AM, 24th Nov                     Brandon Porter                    recipe SWR1465 - The beef crumble has a case price of 202.00 @ 8/5lbs bags  but when i use 5 pounds in recipe it says ingredient cost is $14           10:38 AM, 24th Nov                     Brandon Porter                    ing 110566           10:38 AM, 24th Nov                     Brandon Porter                    sorry that was stock number           10:38 AM, 24th Nov                     Brandon Porter                    ING # 100020           10:39 AM, 24th Nov                     Brandon Porter                    it prices like this in other recipes i see as well           10:41 AM, 24th Nov                   Terri                   Could you add a serving measure on the general tab of the ingredient?  I would add 1 bag is 2267.96 grams           10:41 AM, 24th Nov                   Terri                   Then add 1 bag to recipe instead of 5 pounds           10:42 AM, 24th Nov                     Brandon Porter                    ok           10:42 AM, 24th Nov                     Brandon Porter                    ok, i did not change           10:43 AM, 24th Nov                     Brandon Porter                    *it           10:43 AM, 24th Nov                   Terri                   Also, click on the pencil for the 1 ounce and change to 28.3495 grams           10:43 AM, 24th Nov                     Brandon Porter                    I can't edit that one it says it is converted from 2.2ounce which i pulled oz and gram from manufacturer's nutrition label           10:45 AM, 24th Nov                   Terri                   I see that now...Try changing under ingredients on the recipe by adding 1 bag instead of 5 pound           10:46 AM, 24th Nov                     Brandon Porter                    thats what u already had me do           10:47 AM, 24th Nov                   Terri                   ok, hang on - click on the pencil for the 2.2 ounce and change the grams to 62.369           10:48 AM, 24th Nov                     Brandon Porter                    ok done           10:49 AM, 24th Nov                   Terri                            10:52 AM, 24th Nov                   Terri                   looks better           10:52 AM, 24th Nov                     Brandon Porter                    I figured it out thanks any way. fyi UNDER BUYING GUIDE           10:52 AM, 24th Nov                     Brandon Porter                    THERE WAS ANOTHER SPOT WITH A LOCAL PRICE ENTERED AND THAT PRICE WAS SET AT $100 SOMETHING I DELTED IT AND IT CORRECTED           10:53 AM, 24th Nov                     Brandon Porter                    sorry about the caps           10:53 AM, 24th Nov                   Terri                   great           10:53 AM, 24th Nov                   Terri                   no problem glad it got figured out!           10:53 AM, 24th Nov                                          Take to Team Inbox
turns.body_html:
Brandon Porter
Any one here to help?
10:35 AM, 24th Nov
Jared
Thank you for contacting customer care. I hope you're having a great day!
10:36 AM, 24th Nov
Jared
What can I do for you today
10:36 AM, 24th Nov
Brandon Porter
i have an ingredient in a recipe that wont price correctly
10:36 AM, 24th Nov
Terri
Hi Brandon - What is the ingredient code? And the recipe code?
10:37 AM, 24th Nov
Brandon Porter
recipe SWR1465 - The beef crumble has a case price of 202.00 @ 8/5lbs bags but when i use 5 pounds in recipe it says ingredient cost is $14
10:38 AM, 24th Nov
Brandon Porter
ing 110566
10:38 AM, 24th Nov
Brandon Porter
sorry that was stock number
10:38 AM, 24th Nov
Brandon Porter
ING # 100020
10:39 AM, 24th Nov
Brandon Porter
it prices like this in other recipes i see as well
10:41 AM, 24th Nov
Terri
Could you add a serving measure on the general tab of the ingredient? I would add 1 bag is 2267.96 grams
10:41 AM, 24th Nov
Terri
Then add 1 bag to recipe instead of 5 pounds
10:42 AM, 24th Nov
Brandon Porter
ok
10:42 AM, 24th Nov
Brandon Porter
ok, i did not change
10:43 AM, 24th Nov
Brandon Porter
*it
10:43 AM, 24th Nov
Terri
Also, click on the pencil for the 1 ounce and change to 28.3495 grams
10:43 AM, 24th Nov
Brandon Porter
I can't edit that one it says it is converted from 2.2ounce which i pulled oz and gram from manufacturer's nutrition label
10:45 AM, 24th Nov
Terri
I see that now...Try changing under ingredients on the recipe by adding 1 bag instead of 5 pound
10:46 AM, 24th Nov
Brandon Porter
thats what u already had me do
10:47 AM, 24th Nov
Terri
ok, hang on - click on the pencil for the 2.2 ounce and change the grams to 62.369
10:48 AM, 24th Nov
Brandon Porter
ok done
10:49 AM, 24th Nov
Terri
10:52 AM, 24th Nov
Terri
looks better
10:52 AM, 24th Nov
Brandon Porter
I figured it out thanks any way. fyi UNDER BUYING GUIDE
10:52 AM, 24th Nov
Brandon Porter
THERE WAS ANOTHER SPOT WITH A LOCAL PRICE ENTERED AND THAT PRICE WAS SET AT $100 SOMETHING I DELTED IT AND IT CORRECTED
10:53 AM, 24th Nov
Brandon Porter
sorry about the caps
10:53 AM, 24th Nov
Terri
great
10:53 AM, 24th Nov
Terri
no problem glad it got figured out!
10:53 AM, 24th Nov
Take to Team Inbox

turns.body_text:

 Hello, 
   
 Conducting some integration clean up, we need the following actions to be completed to in the Oklahoma data source. Please complete ASAP. 
   
 1.  change ingredient # to have a ZZ in front of it

3.  remove verification of ingredients 
4.  unlink stock item 
   
   



 
   
 
 
   
 
 
 
Kelly J Caruso, RD, CDN

 Tech & Services 
 Sodexo North America 
   
 518-209-2360

turns.body_text: The stock item and the ingredient had the duplicate Link Records with both Active status. Hence was showing twice on the recipes.      Cleaned up the dupe record, and its now resolved.

The stock item and the ingredient had the duplicate Link Records with both Active status. Hence was showing twice on the recipes. 

Cleaned up the dupe record, and its now resolved.

Hello,

1.  deactivate stock items

Kelly J Caruso, RD, CDN (she/her/hers)

subject: Rio Vista ISD - broken units

turns.author: Karina Farias <kfarias@rvisd.net>
turns.body_text: Karina Farias                    Keith I have a question. When it comes to products. I have to Add New Item first, and then from there, is when you turn it into an ingredient correct? Is that correct step process?           10:59 AM, 14th Jan                     Karina Farias                    and how can I edit this highlighted part?           11:04 AM, 14th Jan                   Keith                   You go in order, Ingredient, Recipe;           11:04 AM, 14th Jan                     Karina Farias                    I already tried "edit units" but it wont let me change it           11:04 AM, 14th Jan                   Keith                   you cant edit those           11:04 AM, 14th Jan                     Karina Farias                    So do I just create a new one and put that one as inactive?           11:05 AM, 14th Jan                   Keith                   Only we can and we do not  like to do that. We suggest making a whole new one, yes           11:05 AM, 14th Jan                   Keith                   We do not allow the changing of broken units because it causes discrepancies in the system. If the stock item is used in inventory, changing the broken units per whole unit will severely affect the perpetual inventory, past transactions and reports in an irreversible way. Although the effect in the production module is not as severe, Food used and Food prepared values in Production records could be different for records before and after the change.  For the above reasons, we strongly advise not change broken units per case.           11:06 AM, 14th Jan                     Karina Farias                    thank you for clarifying that           11:12 AM, 14th Jan                                          Take to Team Inbox
turns.body_html:
Karina Farias
Keith I have a question. When it comes to products. I have to Add New Item first, and then from there, is when you turn it into an ingredient correct? Is that correct step process?
10:59 AM, 14th Jan
Karina Farias
and how can I edit this highlighted part?
11:04 AM, 14th Jan
Keith
You go in order, Ingredient, Recipe;
11:04 AM, 14th Jan
Karina Farias
I already tried "edit units" but it wont let me change it
11:04 AM, 14th Jan
Keith
you cant edit those
11:04 AM, 14th Jan
Karina Farias
So do I just create a new one and put that one as inactive?
11:05 AM, 14th Jan
Keith
Only we can and we do not like to do that. We suggest making a whole new one, yes
11:05 AM, 14th Jan
Keith
We do not allow the changing of broken units because it causes discrepancies in the system. If the stock item is used in inventory, changing the broken units per whole unit will severely affect the perpetual inventory, past transactions and reports in an irreversible way. Although the effect in the production module is not as severe, Food used and Food prepared values in Production records could be different for records before and after the change.
For the above reasons, we strongly advise not change broken units per case.
11:06 AM, 14th Jan
Karina Farias
thank you for clarifying that
11:12 AM, 14th Jan
Take to Team Inbox

subject: [EXT]Production Record weights

turns.author: Meshala Huff <meshala.huff@paisd.org>
turns.body_text:
CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.

Good afternoon,

I am currently in the process of fixing a database of recipes, and I need to understand why some of the weights populate on the production recipe and why some do not. Below is an example:

[image.png]


[image.png]

[image.png]

This is Wilson's production record for 1/12/2026. The quantity prepared shows the pounds and bags for Asian Food Solutions, but that information does not translate for the Mandarin Chicken. Can you assist in troubleshooting shy the data does not correlate?



Meshala Huff
Assistant Director of Child Nutrition
Email: meshala.huff@paisd.org<mailto:meshala.huff@paisd.org>
Phone: 409-984-6231
Cell: 409-960-2745


[https://docs.google.com/uc?export=download&id=1iB96cofisKm2biv3Gv3rOXoUeyyED1M0&revid=0BxpbCjB5yQoOM25uVDBPSTVCKzFvZ3pNU1V4QUNuY3pGaUhvPQ]


CONFIDENTIALITY NOTICE:  This message from the Port Arthur Independent School District may contain confidential or privileged information. If you received this transmission in error, please return it to the sender and delete the material from any computer. Disclosure or use of any part of this message by persons other than the intended recipient is prohibited.

CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.
Good afternoon,

I am currently in the process of fixing a database of recipes, and I need to understand why some of the weights populate on the production recipe and why some do not. Below is an example:






This is Wilson's production record for 1/12/2026. The quantity prepared shows the pounds and bags for Asian Food Solutions, but that information does not translate for the Mandarin Chicken. Can you assist in troubleshooting shy the data does not correlate?



Meshala Huff
Assistant Director of Child Nutrition

Phone: 409-984-6231
Cell: 409-960-2745

turns.author: "customercare@primeroedge.com" <customercare@primeroedge.com>
turns.body_text:
Hello Meshala,  I took a look at both recipes with their ingredients in mind and found the reason the General Tso's shows the weight and the mandarin chicken does not. LI101251 under the buying guide tab there is the stock item tied to the ingredient and under the broken unit weight there is a box toggle where it is left unchecked currently and this is what needs to be enabled in order for the weight to be shown on production records and hit save. The other chicken has it check marked and is the reason it shows up in production as shown below. Let me know if you have questions. Thank you.               Check out PrimeroEdge Training WEBINARS Available: Click Here  SchoolCafe Training WEBINARS Available: Click Here     Let us know if you have any questions regarding this ticket by

turns.body_html:
Hello Meshala,
I took a look at both recipes with their ingredients in mind and found the reason the General Tso's shows the weight and the mandarin chicken does not. LI101251 under the buying guide tab there is the stock item tied to the ingredient and under the broken unit weight there is a box toggle where it is left unchecked currently and this is what needs to be enabled in order for the weight to be shown on production records and hit save. The other chicken has it check marked and is the reason it shows up in production as shown below. Let me know if you have questions. Thank you.

subject: Metz-[EXT]Whole Milk and Primero

turns.author: Michelle Martucci <mmartucci@metzcorp.com>
turns.body_text:
CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.

Good afternoon-
With the Gov't releasing new guidelines stating schools can now use whole milk in schools effective immediately, and that the saturated fat from the milk will no longer count toward the 10% limit on saturated fat in analysis, do you have any guidance on when Primero will have the capability to exclude milk from saturated fat limits?  We have districts asking about this, and am not sure how to respond.  Any info or updates is appreciated.


--
Michelle Martucci, RDN, LDN
Corporate K-12 Registered Dietitian
Metz Culinary Management
Two Woodland Drive, Dallas, PA  18612
Cell: 610-295-7903
mmartucci@metzcorp.com<mailto:mmartucci@metzcorp.com>

--
Michelle Martucci, RDN, LDN
Corporate K-12 Registered Dietitian
Metz Culinary Management 
Two Woodland Drive, Dallas, PA  18612

mmartucci@metzcorp.com

turns.author: "customercare@primeroedge.com" <customercare@primeroedge.com>
turns.body_text:
Hello Michelle,     We are aware of the new Milk Guidelines that were signed on January 14, 2026, and have been in communication with the USDA regarding the implementation. They have confirmed that the changes only impact lunch menus at this time, and we have been advised that all approved software developers will be receiving directives soon in regards to these changes.  We will be working to implement the changes as soon as we receive that directive.  Please watch the release notes for information regarding the version containing the change.  The changes will retroactively apply to the menu analysis since the ruling specified that it is for immediate adoption and use.         Check out PrimeroEdge Training WEBINARS Available: Click Here  SchoolCafe Training WEBINARS Available: Click Here     Let us know if you have any questions regarding this ticket by
replying all to this ticket          Thank You      Francesca Benson |  CYBERSOFT  Region Lead Customer Care Specialist   PrimeroEdge | SchoolCafé  Support: 1.866.442.6030  |  Email: customercare@primeroedge.com      Let’s Chat …Send an email to CustomerCare@primeroedge.com to enable your PrimeroEdge support LIVE CHAT feature      Get ready for back to school with a refresher training check out PrimeroEdge Academy
turns.body_html:
Hello Michelle,

We are aware of the new Milk Guidelines that were signed on January 14, 2026, and have been in communication with the USDA regarding the implementation. They have confirmed that the changes only impact lunch menus at this time, and we have been advised that all approved software developers will be receiving directives soon in regards to these changes.

We will be working to implement the changes as soon as we receive that directive.

Please watch the release notes for information regarding the version containing the change.  The changes will retroactively apply to the menu analysis since the ruling specified that it is for immediate adoption and use.

turns.body_text:
CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.
 
 
 
 
 
Could you advise on the question below regarding whole milk not being an option in Menu Pattern/Planning?  
 
 
 
 
Thanks, Kurt 
 
 
 ---------- Forwarded message --------- 
From: Kim Walls 
<kim.walls@msdmartinsville.org> 
Date: Fri, Feb 13, 2026 at 9:50 AM 
Subject: whole milk 
To: Kurt Bodell <kurt.bodell@msdmartinsville.org> 
 
 
 
 
 Kurt, 
 
 
 
 I've entered the whole milk into PE under inventory, ingredients and recipes.  However, the recipe wants me to choose a milk type on the menu tab.  Whole milk is not an option on the drop down box.    Is this something we need to ask PE to add, or do we
 make the change somewhere under the configuration?  I haven't looked hard, but it's something we need to fix either way. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Thanks,
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Kim Walls

Hello Branden,  I took a look at the recipe in question and when i pull the recipe report, it seems to be pulling for 80 hot packs with the 80 hot dogs and 320 pieces for the potato stars (which would be 80 servings of 4 a piece). The nutrients is pulling through as listed on the ingredients for their serving 130 from potato stars and 100 for the hot dog to be 230 Cal  total which is reflected on the recipe report. The only thing I see that would need to be corrected is under the recipe code LR102318  where you have the potato stars as 320 for the ingredient which is correct and 3 for the stock item which would cause issues so you would want to update that to 320 so it pulls the correct amount when using it in production from your inventory. turns.body_text:
Hello Branden,     In looking into the math, you have multiple things going on that aren't "math-ing" correctly.     On the ingredient - you have one case listed as over 10,000 grams.  In the inventory item, you have one case listed as containing 128 units at 3 ounces per unit.      The inventory name of the product says there are 6 bags at 4 pounds each... which does not match up with the 128 broken units of "1 each"     Does each of the 6 bags contain 128 smiley face potatoes?   So that "Broken Units" should be 6 Bags, and each broken unit (1 Bag) should be 2176 grams?   (17 grams each potato X 128 per bag based on the ingredient conversion of 4 pieces = 68 grams)     Once all of the numbers are set to correspond with each other correctly, everything will pull the way it's expected. Right now, I think the software may be confused due to the data entered.           Let us know if you have any questions regarding this ticket by
replying all to this ticket         Schrenia Bockholt | CYBERSOFT  Business Analyst, Product Management  PrimeroEdge | SchoolCafé
turns.body_html:
Hello Branden,

In looking into the math, you have multiple things going on that aren't "math-ing" correctly.

On the ingredient - you have one case listed as over 10,000 grams.
In the inventory item, you have one case listed as containing 128 units at 3 ounces per unit. 

The inventory name of the product says there are 6 bags at 4 pounds each... which does not match up with the 128 broken units of "1 each"

Does each of the 6 bags contain 128 smiley face potatoes? 
So that "Broken Units" should be 6 Bags, and each broken unit (1 Bag) should be 2176 grams? 
(17 grams each potato X 128 per bag based on the ingredient conversion of 4 pieces = 68 grams)

Once all of the numbers are set to correspond with each other correctly, everything will pull the way it's expected. Right now, I think the software may be confused due to the data entered.



Let us know if you have any questions regarding this ticket by replying all to this ticket 

Schrenia Bockholt | CYBERSOFT
Business Analyst, Product Management
PrimeroEdge | SchoolCafé

turns.author: "customercare@primeroedge.com" <customercare@primeroedge.com>
turns.body_text:
Hello Lindsey,  The relationship between Whole and Broken Units is more complicated than it may appear. In the backend, we convert whole units to broken units in order to perform important calculations that involve quantity and price. Maintaining the Broken Units per Whole is important in order to preserve data accuracy and maintain inventory quantities. If Broken Units per Whole is changed for a particular item, all historical data for that item becomes inaccurate. Where you may have had 1.5 WU before, if you change Broken Units per Whole that 1.5 WU could become 2 WU. This means Inventory Valuations, Transfers, Reports, and more become unreliable and may cause all sorts of discrepancies, which will only bring about more issues that are even harder to identify.     Put simply, if an item has had any transactions with Inventory, it could be very damaging to change the Broken Units. It is for this reason that we strongly recommend creating a new item with the different Broken Units per Whole value, so the old item can be managed accurately until all remaining quantities are depleted and the item is no longer used.     With that in mind, would you like for us to go and change the broken unit?  Thank you,     Check out PrimeroEdge Training WEBINARS Available: Click Here  SchoolCafe Training WEBINARS Available: Click Here    Let us know if you have any questions regarding this ticket by
replying all to this ticket        Jordan King | CYBERSOFT  call: 866.442.6030   email: customercare@primeroedge.com   www.primeroedge.com  YOUR GOALS ARE OUR GOALS.  Let’s Chat …Send an email to CustomerCare@primeroedge.com to enable your PrimeroEdge support LIVE CHAT feature  Check out our refresher training courses in the PrimeroEdge Academy

This means Inventory Valuations, Transfers, Reports, and more become unreliable and may cause all sorts of discrepancies, which will only bring about more issues that are even harder to identify. 

Put simply, if an item has had any transactions with Inventory, it could be very damaging to change the Broken Units. It is for this reason that we strongly recommend creating a new item with the different Broken Units per Whole value, so the old item can be managed accurately until all remaining quantities are depleted and the item is no longer used.

With that in mind, would you like for us to go and change the broken unit?

Thank you,

Thank you for this information. I will create a new item. Thank you. 


On Mon, Mar 2, 2026 at 12:11 PM customercare@primeroedge.com <customercare@primeroedge.com> wrote:
Hello Lindsey,

The relationship between Whole and Broken Units is more complicated than it may appear. In the backend, we convert whole units to broken units in order to perform important calculations that involve quantity and price. Maintaining the Broken Units per Whole is important in order to preserve data accuracy and maintain inventory quantities. If Broken Units per Whole is changed for a particular item, all historical data for that item becomes inaccurate. Where you may have had 1.5 WU before, if you change Broken Units per Whole that 1.5 WU could become 2 WU. This means Inventory Valuations, Transfers, Reports, and more become unreliable and may cause all sorts of discrepancies, which will only bring about more issues that are even harder to identify. 

Put simply, if an item has had any transactions with Inventory, it could be very damaging to change the Broken Units. It is for this reason that we strongly recommend creating a new item with the different Broken Units per Whole value, so the old item can be managed accurately until all remaining quantities are depleted and the item is no longer used.

With that in mind, would you like for us to go and change the broken unit?

Thank you,

turns.author: agent:22034350878
turns.body_text: Stacie called now that back from vacation. Her person who handles inventory is out.  Walked her through where to click "Edit Stock Item" and make the update for the weight to Bag weight   (as they have it setup for 2 bags per case and a BKU is really 1 bag (where 53pieces = 1 bag, and they setup their BKU sizing as "53 eaches" so the weight needed to be 53X1.5 oz - 79.5oz)    Once updated the pricing displays correctly.  Also 79.5 oz converts to 2253.787g /53 = 42.52428g as ingredient has.
turns.body_html:
Stacie called now that back from vacation. Her person who handles inventory is out.
Walked her through where to click "Edit Stock Item" and make the update for the weight to Bag weight 
(as they have it setup for 2 bags per case and a BKU is really 1 bag (where 53pieces = 1 bag, and they setup their BKU sizing as "53 eaches" so the weight needed to be 53X1.5 oz - 79.5oz)  
Once updated the pricing displays correctly.  Also 79.5 oz converts to 2253.787g /53 = 42.52428g as ingredient has.

turns.author: "customercare@primeroedge.com" <customercare@primeroedge.com>
turns.body_text:
Hello Stacie,     Thank you for your call and welcome back! Glad we were able to walkthrough where to make the correction on the stock item weight via Edit Stock Item to be the bag weight since the Broken unit was really 1 bag (aka 53 pieces).  Once revised, you confirmed the pricing was showing as expected now and the information was flowing through as expected (1 serving/each = 42.525g, 53 each x 1.5oz or 4.525g = 1 bag (53 pieces) at 79.5oz with 2 bags per case)     We will close this ticket, but should you have any further questions or issues regarding this let us know by replying all to this ticket      Have a great rest of your day!      Check out PrimeroEdge Training WEBINARS Available: Click Here  SchoolCafe Training WEBINARS Available: Click Here        Thank you,  CarrieLee Sparks | CYBERSOFT  Customer Care Specialist, Customer and Expert Care  Support: 866.442.6030 | Email: customercare@primeroedge.com   PrimeroEdge | SchoolCafé  Check out our refresher
training courses in the Primeroedge Academy
turns.body_html:
Hello Stacie,

Thank you for your call and welcome back! Glad we were able to walkthrough where to make the correction on the stock item weight via Edit Stock Item to be the bag weight since the Broken unit was really 1 bag (aka 53 pieces).  Once revised, you confirmed the pricing was showing as expected now and the information was flowing through as expected (1 serving/each = 42.525g, 53 each x 1.5oz or 4.525g = 1 bag (53 pieces) at 79.5oz with 2 bags per case)

We will close this ticket, but should you have any further questions or issues regarding this let us know by replying all to this ticket

subject: PE Implementation - Warren County Schools - District would like all their local ingredients removed

turns.author: Terri Mullican <mullicant1@warrenschools.com>
turns.body_text: Good morning,     Warren County had 760 ingredients imported on 8/7/25 (see ticket #290044) for reference.  They are requesting all the ingredients be removed because they'd like to start with fresh data.
turns.body_html:
Good morning,

Warren County had 760 ingredients imported on 8/7/25 (see ticket #290044) for reference.  They are requesting all the ingredients be removed because they'd like to start with fresh data.

turns.author: agent:22010986567
turns.body_text: Adding a note - we highly discourage them from using other district's data, but they insisted.  If they choose to use another district's data we do not get involved.  They reach out to the district and request their data.  In this instance it seems they simply changed their mind now that they realized this isn't the correct ingredients and will need to start over with entering their own ingredients.  Please remove all local ingredients.

subject: OL1 - Bland ISD - Menu Planning Question

turns.author: Tracy White <tracy.white@blandisd.org>
turns.body_text: Tracy called to learn how to change the default ingredient measurement from 3/4 cup to 1 cup, and Chris guided her through creating a single‑ingredient recipe with a 1‑cup serving size, which resolved the issue.  Recap   Tracy explained that the default measurement for ingredients in her menu planner is 3/4 cup and she wants it to default to 1 cup for all vegetables and fruits.  Chris accessed the system, identified the broccoli ingredient codes (LI 100241 and LI 100181) and confirmed the default serving size could be changed.  Tracy created a single‑ingredient recipe for broccoli, set the serving size to 1 cup, saved it, and saw the change reflected in the nutrient view.  Tracy added the new broccoli recipe to a high‑school lunch menu, verified it appeared as 1 cup, and confirmed the drop‑down now allows selecting 1 cup.  Chris confirmed that using single‑ingredient recipes with a 1‑cup serving size is the correct approach for all fresh vegetables and fruits.
turns.body_html:
Tracy called to learn how to change the default ingredient measurement from 3/4 cup to 1 cup, and Chris guided her through creating a single‑ingredient recipe with a 1‑cup serving size, which resolved the issue.
Recap
  • Tracy explained that the default measurement for ingredients in her menu planner is 3/4 cup and she wants it to default to 1 cup for all vegetables and fruits.
  • Chris accessed the system, identified the broccoli ingredient codes (LI 100241 and LI 100181) and confirmed the default serving size could be changed.
  • Tracy created a single‑ingredient recipe for broccoli, set the serving size to 1 cup, saved it, and saw the change reflected in the nutrient view.
  • Tracy added the new broccoli recipe to a high‑school lunch menu, verified it appeared as 1 cup, and confirmed the drop‑down now allows selecting 1 cup.
  • Chris confirmed that using single‑ingredient recipes with a 1‑cup serving size is the correct approach for all fresh vegetables and fruits.

turns.author: "customercare@primeroedge.com" <customercare@primeroedge.com>
turns.body_text:
Hello Sherry,        To fix this, search for any ingredients you’re missing. Go to Menu Planning > Recipes, then enter the Recipe Code or Recipe Name. Make sure the “Include Discontinued Recipes” option is checked, and then activate the recipe. 763-506-1241

turns.author: "customercare@primeroedge.com" <customercare@primeroedge.com>
turns.body_text:
Hello Allison,     There are two ways to import ingredients:  1) Menu Planning-->Ingredients-->Press on "Add New Ingredient" then input the information needed  2) There is an import and export function where you can download an excel template and input the data that way and then import the file to PE.           - This could be found in Menu Planning-->Configuration-->Import/Export                         Check out PrimeroEdge Training WEBINARS Available: Click Here  SchoolCafe Training WEBINARS Available: Click Here     Let us know if you have any questions regarding this ticket by
replying all to this ticket
turns.body_html:
Hello Allison,

There are two ways to import ingredients:
1) Menu Planning-->Ingredients-->Press on "Add New Ingredient" then input the information needed
2) There is an import and export function where you can download an excel template and input the data that way and then import the file to PE. 
        - This could be found in Menu Planning-->Configuration-->Import/Export

763-506-1241

I hope you're having a great day!           10:12 AM, 14th Apr                   Jared                   It may not be the item itself, the red is an indicator that either the recipe itself, one of the ingredients, or the stock item the ingredient is linked to is inactive           10:13 AM, 14th Apr                   Jared                   In this case, let me check this item           10:13 AM, 14th Apr                     Amy Godwin                    None are inactive           10:13 AM, 14th Apr                   Jared                   Where is it showing as red, the stock item is fine, but what is the recipe on your menu that is red           10:14 AM, 14th Apr                   Jared                   I need to search the recipe and ingredients as well           10:14 AM, 14th Apr                     Amy Godwin                    LI100002 ingredient, no recipe. It's a bag of chips           10:15 AM, 14th Apr                   Jared                   That ingredient is inactive           10:15 AM, 14th Apr                   Jared                            10:15 AM, 14th Apr                   Jared                   Is active is unchecked           10:15 AM, 14th Apr                   Jared                   same for li10000003           10:15 AM, 14th Apr                   Jared                   both have inactive ingredients           10:16 AM, 14th Apr                   Jared                   the baked BBQ chips too           10:16 AM, 14th Apr                   Jared                   the stock item may have been, but red indicates something is inactive along the way           10:16 AM, 14th Apr                     Amy Godwin                    So they don't just go in as a stock item since it does not have a recipe?           10:16 AM, 14th Apr                   Jared                   it does have a recipe           10:16 AM, 14th Apr                     Amy Godwin                    Got it, Thanks.           10:16 AM, 14th Apr                   Jared                   Its a single ingredient recipe           10:16 AM, 14th Apr                   Jared                   Items like chips count as single ingredient recipes, meaning they are both an ingredient and the entire recipe itself           10:18 AM, 14th Apr                   Jared                   Within the recipe, you then link it all to the stock item, that is where the connect is broken in the case of the chips:           10:19 AM, 14th Apr                     Amy Godwin                    Thank you           10:19 AM, 14th Apr                     Amy Godwin                    No clue how those ever became inactive, but I'm newer to this district. Glad to know to look for that red           10:20 AM, 14th Apr                   Jared                   If you wanted to find out, all items should show that within their item history, but that is only really if you care to know           10:21 AM, 14th Apr                   Jared                   Who made them active/inactive I mean           10:21 AM, 14th Apr                     Amy Godwin                    Is their a way to generate a list of all?           10:22 AM, 14th Apr                   Jared                   Unfortunately not at this time, I can see if it would be possible to create something like a dynamic query           10:23 AM, 14th Apr                     Amy Godwin                    OK. We are always here if you need           10:27 AM, 14th Apr                                          Take to Team Inbox
turns.body_html:
Amy Godwin
Can you please remind me why ingredients or menu items are showing up in red? I know it was something with the last update but can not remember
10:05 AM, 14th Apr
Amy Godwin
We are still having items show up red that are active, is their another reason they would be red?
10:08 AM, 14th Apr
Amy Godwin
An example would be P6602001 and actually all are baked chips are showing up red even though they are active
10:09 AM, 14th Apr
Jared
Thank you for contacting customer care. I hope you're having a great day!
10:12 AM, 14th Apr
Jared
It may not be the item itself, the red is an indicator that either the recipe itself, one of the ingredients, or the stock item the ingredient is linked to is inactive
10:13 AM, 14th Apr
Jared
In this case, let me check this item
10:13 AM, 14th Apr
Amy Godwin
None are inactive
10:13 AM, 14th Apr
Jared
Where is it showing as red, the stock item is fine, but what is the recipe on your menu that is red
10:14 AM, 14th Apr
Jared
I need to search the recipe and ingredients as well
10:14 AM, 14th Apr
Amy Godwin
LI100002 ingredient, no recipe. It's a bag of chips
10:15 AM, 14th Apr
Jared
That ingredient is inactive
10:15 AM, 14th Apr
Jared
10:15 AM, 14th Apr
Jared
Is active is unchecked
10:15 AM, 14th Apr
Jared
same for li10000003
10:15 AM, 14th Apr
Jared
both have inactive ingredients
10:16 AM, 14th Apr
Jared
the baked BBQ chips too
10:16 AM, 14th Apr
Jared
the stock item may have been, but red indicates something is inactive along the way
10:16 AM, 14th Apr
Amy Godwin
So they don't just go in as a stock item since it does not have a recipe?
10:16 AM, 14th Apr
Jared
it does have a recipe
10:16 AM, 14th Apr
Amy Godwin
Got it, Thanks.
10:16 AM, 14th Apr
Jared
Its a single ingredient recipe
10:16 AM, 14th Apr
Jared
Items like chips count as single ingredient recipes, meaning they are both an ingredient and the entire recipe itself
10:18 AM, 14th Apr
Jared
Within the recipe, you then link it all to the stock item, that is where the connect is broken in the case of the chips:
10:19 AM, 14th Apr
Amy Godwin
Thank you
10:19 AM, 14th Apr
Amy Godwin
No clue how those ever became inactive, but I'm newer to this district. Glad to know to look for that red
10:20 AM, 14th Apr
Jared
If you wanted to find out, all items should show that within their item history, but that is only really if you care to know
10:21 AM, 14th Apr
Jared
Who made them active/inactive I mean
10:21 AM, 14th Apr
Amy Godwin
Is their a way to generate a list of all?
10:22 AM, 14th Apr
Jared
Unfortunately not at this time, I can see if it would be possible to create something like a dynamic query
10:23 AM, 14th Apr
Amy Godwin
OK. Thanks for you help
10:25 AM, 14th Apr
Amy Godwin
Have a great day
10:25 AM, 14th Apr
Jared
You as well! We are always here if you need
10:27 AM, 14th Apr
Take to Team Inbox

turns.body_text: Sandy with the district reported an that the buying guide tab is missing from all of the local ingredients in her districts system that she looked up.  We were able to verify the buying guide tab is visible for the shared data base ingredients just not the local as seen below
turns.body_html:
Sandy with the district reported an that the buying guide tab is missing from all of the local ingredients in her districts system that she looked up.
We were able to verify the buying guide tab is visible for the shared data base ingredients just not the local as seen below

turns.author: agent:22001958196
turns.body_text: The district doesn't have the inventory module, so they can't set up vendors for their local items. Confirming with Schrenia that this is the reason why.
turns.body_html: The district doesn't have the inventory module, so they can't set up vendors for their local items. Confirming with Schrenia that this is the reason why.

turns.author: agent:22001958196
turns.body_text: District doesn't have the Buying Guide setting turned on. Emailing district.
turns.body_html: District doesn't have the Buying Guide setting turned on. Emailing district.

turns.author: "customercare@primeroedge.com" <customercare@primeroedge.com>
turns.body_text:
Hello Sandy,     Under System > Management > System Settings, in the ​Menu Planning tab, there is a setting called "Buying Guide - Enable Tab". turns.body_html:
Mike Suttle
I am trying to figure out how to link the stock number to the ingredient. This recipe is for Cheeseburger Mac And Cheese. The nutritionals are for mac and cheese only. I need to tie in the hamburger meat that is being sent from the central kitchen. Item number 1066 is for the beef
01:16 PM, 23rd Apr
Francesca
Good Afternoon Mike,
01:25 PM, 23rd Apr
Francesca
Your stock item can be linked to an ingredient once created under the buying guide tab
01:26 PM, 23rd Apr
Francesca
Please let us know if you still need assistance with this after adding your stock item in your ingredients
01:27 PM, 23rd Apr
Mike Suttle
where is the buying guide tab?
01:28 PM, 23rd Apr
Francesca
what is the ingredient number so that I may pull it up and screen shot this for you
01:28 PM, 23rd Apr
Mike Suttle
the item number is 1066
01:29 PM, 23rd Apr
Francesca
ok, one moment please
01:29 PM, 23rd Apr
Francesca
#1066 is this the ingredient code we need to link a stock item to?
01:33 PM, 23rd Apr
Francesca
01:33 PM, 23rd Apr
Francesca
I see 1066 is the stock item number though we need to ingredient code that the stock item will be linked to
01:34 PM, 23rd Apr
Francesca
01:34 PM, 23rd Apr
Francesca
What is the ingredient?
01:35 PM, 23rd Apr
Mike Suttle
the ingredient is ground beef cooked from central kitchen and being sent as a item to the sites. TR 4090 is for the cheeseburger mac and cheese. The TR for cheeseburger meat being sent is TR4087
01:38 PM, 23rd Apr
Francesca
Stock items are added and linked to the ingredients in recipes.
01:46 PM, 23rd Apr
Francesca
After further research the recipe TR4087 Ingredient LI101159 Beef Fine Ground I am seeing the stock item 9904 already linked. This would have to be done for all ingredients for this data to transfer through to production and central kitchen
01:47 PM, 23rd Apr
Francesca
01:47 PM, 23rd Apr
Francesca
navigate to Menu Planning>Ingredients>Ingredients find your ingredient by searching then, opening them going to the buying guide tab and adding the stock items and saving
01:48 PM, 23rd Apr
Francesca
you will do this for all ingredients you need to link stock items to
01:51 PM, 23rd Apr
Francesca
Please let us know if there is anything else that we can assist you with?
01:56 PM, 23rd Apr
Mike Suttle
okay i am linking ingredients now
01:57 PM, 23rd Apr
Francesca
sounds good
01:58 PM, 23rd Apr
Mike Suttle
It is not pulling the nutritonals from the ground beef being added
02:01 PM, 23rd Apr
Francesca
where is it not pulling the nutritional's to?
02:14 PM, 23rd Apr
Francesca
You may need to resave the recipe and or resend the food required data through to production by clicking on the update food required button in the recipe to the right as seen in the screen shot below
02:16 PM, 23rd Apr
Francesca
02:17 PM, 23rd Apr
Francesca
as long as the nutrients are visible in the ingredients and the recipe then they should pull through once refreshed
02:17 PM, 23rd Apr
Take to Team Inbox

turns.author: Mike Suttles <msuttles@scusd.net>
turns.body_text: Mike Suttle                    trying to figure out why the Taco meat is not showing up on production or the grocery list. TR4269           01:52 PM, 28th Apr                   Jared                   Thank you for contacting customer care. I hope you're having a great day!           02:20 PM, 28th Apr                   Jared                   I can look into it here, let me see           02:20 PM, 28th Apr                   Jared                   It could be related to the conversions for serving sizes, or the ordering info from the relevant items           02:20 PM, 28th Apr                   Jared                   Allow me a little time to research, I can also email you if that is easier           02:21 PM, 28th Apr                     Mike Suttle                    okay           02:43 PM, 28th Apr                     Mike Suttle                    my email is msuttles@scusd.net if you need           02:45 PM, 28th Apr                     Mike Suttle                    Are you able to assist?           03:36 PM, 28th Apr                   Jared                   Yes, my apologies, its a bit hectic around here, I have the item pulled up           03:40 PM, 28th Apr                   Jared                   What day are you looking at the production record or the grocery list for?           03:40 PM, 28th Apr                     Mike Suttle                    May 8th for Briarwood           03:41 PM, 28th Apr                   Jared                   I found it           03:43 PM, 28th Apr                   Jared                   The beef stock item           03:43 PM, 28th Apr                   Jared                   I know not everything highlighted has asterisks but           03:44 PM, 28th Apr                   Jared                   When looking at any ingredient, if you go to the buying guide tab and see this, that is why:           03:44 PM, 28th Apr                   Jared                   It says down below, but without the broken unit data, no calculations can be made           03:45 PM, 28th Apr                   Jared                   As the system needs that for costing and orders and reports and things           03:45 PM, 28th Apr                   Jared                   So all other items are filled in properly, except the beef itself           03:45 PM, 28th Apr                   Jared                   More literally, the recipe calls for 4.5 pounds of the beef, but based on what we see there the beef is ordered in whole bags at a time, and the size and weight of the bag is not listed? Is it a 1lb bag? A 5 lb bag? How does the system know to convert 1 bag to 4.5 pounds when that info is not given?           03:47 PM, 28th Apr                   Jared                   That's why it is required           03:47 PM, 28th Apr                     Mike Suttle                    thanks           03:51 PM, 28th Apr                                          Take to Team Inbox
turns.body_html:
Mike Suttle
trying to figure out why the Taco meat is not showing up on production or the grocery list. TR4269
01:52 PM, 28th Apr
Jared
Thank you for contacting customer care. I hope you're having a great day!
02:20 PM, 28th Apr
Jared
I can look into it here, let me see
02:20 PM, 28th Apr
Jared
It could be related to the conversions for serving sizes, or the ordering info from the relevant items
02:20 PM, 28th Apr
Jared
Allow me a little time to research, I can also email you if that is easier
02:21 PM, 28th Apr
Mike Suttle
okay
02:43 PM, 28th Apr
Mike Suttle
my email is msuttles@scusd.net if you need
02:45 PM, 28th Apr
Mike Suttle
Are you able to assist?
03:36 PM, 28th Apr
Jared
Yes, my apologies, its a bit hectic around here, I have the item pulled up
03:40 PM, 28th Apr
Jared
What day are you looking at the production record or the grocery list for?
03:40 PM, 28th Apr
Mike Suttle
May 8th for Briarwood
03:41 PM, 28th Apr
Jared
I found it
03:43 PM, 28th Apr
Jared
The beef stock item
03:43 PM, 28th Apr
Jared
I know not everything highlighted has asterisks but
03:44 PM, 28th Apr
Jared
When looking at any ingredient, if you go to the buying guide tab and see this, that is why:
03:44 PM, 28th Apr
Jared
It says down below, but without the broken unit data, no calculations can be made
03:45 PM, 28th Apr
Jared
As the system needs that for costing and orders and reports and things
03:45 PM, 28th Apr
Jared
So all other items are filled in properly, except the beef itself
03:45 PM, 28th Apr
Jared
More literally, the recipe calls for 4.5 pounds of the beef, but based on what we see there the beef is ordered in whole bags at a time, and the size and weight of the bag is not listed? Is it a 1lb bag? A 5 lb bag? How does the system know to convert 1 bag to 4.5 pounds when that info is not given?
03:47 PM, 28th Apr
Jared
That's why it is required
03:47 PM, 28th Apr
Mike Suttle
thanks
03:51 PM, 28th Apr

turns.body_text: The district reached out to report that they are still having issues being able to upload documents under Menu Planning>Ingredients>Ingredients - Documents tab - upload   This section does allow you to select a document to upload though once you click the upload button the screen freezes for a while then produces and Internal Server Error message as seen below      The district mentioned that a hotfix was applied that was supposed to fix this issue to their URL though the issue has still not been resolved.     Example Ingredient:     LI-100746 Milk Shelf Stable

subject: PrimeroEdge Sodexo - Sodexo School Services - Alaska Ingredients to ZZ

turns.author: Kelly Caruso <kelly.caruso1@sodexo.com>
turns.body_text:
CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.
Hello,

Conducting some integration clean up, we need the following actions to be completed to in the Alaska data source. Please complete ASAP.

1.  change ingredient # to have a ZZ in front of it
2.  discontinue ingredients
3.  remove verification of ingredients
4.  unlink stock item



Ingredients to ZZ see attachment





Kelly J Caruso, RD, CDN (she/her/hers)
Manager Nutrition/Menu Service
Tech & Services
Sodexo North America

518-209-2360
Kelly.caruso1@sodexo.com


[Sodexo logo with tagline - It all starts with the everyday]<https://us.sodexo.com/home.html>[cid:image003.png@01DCE793.AE45C8D0]


[A close-up of a button  Description automatically generated]<https://sodexo-ism.ivanticloud.com/Modules/SelfService/#serviceCatalog/request/A76E12F86DCC4896901F17E2F6B6AE9D>

Hello,
 
Conducting some integration clean up, we need the following actions to be completed to in the Alaska data source. Please complete ASAP.

3.  remove verification of ingredients
4.  unlink stock item

Kelly J Caruso, RD, CDN (she/her/hers)
